old_claim.note: Plant Domestication provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Stored-seed selection contributes to the domestication process, making fully domesticated plants an outcome rather than a prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Review discussion: domestication changes require sowing seed stored from an earlier harvest, while harvesting, tillage, and processing impose additional selection pressures over a long cultivation process.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Repeated storage and sowing of harvested seed contributed to domestication; fully domesticated plants were an outcome, not a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. Stored-seed selection contributes to the domestication process, making fully domesticated plants an outcome rather than a prerequisite.

ontology_before: selective_seed_saving treated plant_domestication as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: selective_seed_saving is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims plant_domestication as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: selective_seed_saving no longer directly depends on plant_domestication.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current selective_seed_saving scope technically requires plant_domestication.
short_reason: Stored-seed selection contributes to the domestication process, making fully domesticated plants an outcome rather than a prerequisite.
